title: Hemingway's 60 Polydactyl Cats Still Living 🐱
description:
The genetic and historical truth behind the Hemingway House polydactyl cat colony in Key West. Documented research on the Pd gene mutation, the 1935 origin, and the legally protected modern colony of 60+ six-toed cats.

Sources: Hemingway Home Museum records, Journal of Heredity polydactyly studies, Lyons Lab UC Davis feline genetics, USDA exhibitor regulations, AVMA breed standards.

#PolydactylCats #HemingwayCats #CatGenetics #SixToedCats

segments.voice: 60 polydactyl cats currently live in Ernest Hemingway's Key West home, descendants of his original six-toed cat Snow White from 1935. These cats have legal protection under USDA exhibitor regulations and represent the world's most documented polydactyl colony. The genetic story behind them is extraordinary.
segments.visual: polydactyl cat hemingway house key west colony

segments.voice: Polydactyly in cats results from a dominant autosomal mutation on the Sonic Hedgehog gene. The Lyons Lab at UC Davis confirmed in 2008 research that only one parent needs to carry the Pd gene for offspring to express extra toes. This high heritability explains rapid colony establishment from single founder cats.
segments.visual: cat genetics laboratory pd gene mutation diagram

segments.voice: Snow White was given to Ernest Hemingway by a ship captain named Stanley Dexter in 1935. The original cat had six toes on each front paw. Sailors considered polydactyl cats lucky and frequently kept them aboard ships, which explains the higher prevalence of these cats in port towns globally.
segments.visual: ernest hemingway with original snow white cat 1935

segments.voice: Normal cat paws have 18 toes total. Five on each front paw and four on each back paw. Polydactyl cats can have up to 28 toes documented in extreme cases. The Hemingway colony averages 22 to 24 toes per cat, with extra digits primarily on the front paws.
segments.visual: normal cat paw versus polydactyl paw comparison anatomical

segments.voice: The Hemingway House Museum maintains the cats under USDA exhibitor license number 58-C-1062. This legal classification provides federal protection requiring specific care standards. Hurricane evacuation plans, dedicated veterinary care, and breeding management are all federally regulated for this specific colony.
segments.visual: hemingway house museum legal documentation usda license

segments.voice: Maine Coon cats originally exhibited polydactyly at high rates due to similar maritime origins. The Cat Fanciers Association banned polydactyly from breed standards in 1953, eliminating the trait from registered Maine Coons. The PolyTraks organization preserves polydactyl Maine Coon lineage outside mainstream cat registries.
segments.visual: polydactyl maine coon historical photographs preservation

segments.voice: The geographic distribution of polydactyl cats follows historical shipping routes. Concentrations exist in Boston, Halifax, Cardiff, and the western coast of England. Genetic mapping from Lyons Lab in 2014 traced the Pd gene back to a single founder population in Wales during the medieval period.
segments.visual: world map polydactyl cat distribution shipping routes

segments.voice: Polydactyly does not affect cat health negatively when properly expressed. The Hemingway colony shows no increased rates of arthritis, walking difficulty, or paw injuries compared to standard-toed cats. The extra digits function normally and provide enhanced grip on climbing surfaces and prey capture mechanics.
segments.visual: polydactyl cat climbing tree using extra toes efficiently

segments.voice: The Pd gene shows incomplete penetrance, meaning not all offspring of polydactyl parents inherit visible extra toes. Approximately 70 percent of kittens born to one polydactyl parent express the trait. The Hemingway colony maintains the trait through this Mendelian inheritance pattern across generations.
segments.visual: polydactyl kittens different toe counts genetic variation

segments.voice: Three distinct polydactyl patterns have been documented. Pre-axial polydactyly adds toes on the thumb side, creating mitten-paw appearance. Post-axial polydactyly adds toes on the pinkie side. Central polydactyly creates intermediate digit duplication. The Hemingway colony shows primarily pre-axial expression in modern generations.
segments.visual: three polydactyl pattern types comparison diagram

segments.voice: Historical references to polydactyl cats appear in maritime logs dating to the 1600s. The British Royal Navy specifically valued these cats for their perceived hunting advantages and superstitious luck status. Ships transporting these cats to Massachusetts ports established the New England polydactyl population that persists today.
segments.visual: maritime history polydactyl cats ship documentation

segments.voice: Theodore Roosevelt owned a polydactyl cat named Slippers who lived in the White House. The cat had six toes on each foot and famously interrupted state dinners. This connection to American presidential history occurred decades before Hemingway received his first polydactyl cat from Captain Dexter in Key West.
segments.visual: theodore roosevelt slippers polydactyl cat white house historical

segments.voice: Snowshoe and lynx cats display natural polydactyl-like adaptation. Their large paws with snow-shoeing ability provided evolutionary advantage in deep snow environments. This convergent adaptation occurred independently from the Pd gene mutation, demonstrating multiple evolutionary paths toward enhanced paw surface area.
segments.visual: lynx large paws snow walking adaptation comparison

segments.voice: The Hemingway estate honors the cats through tradition and naming. All cats in the colony receive names of famous celebrities, authors, or historical figures. Current residents include cats named after Charlie Chaplin, Audrey Hepburn, and Mark Twain, continuing the literary heritage of the original property.
segments.visual: hemingway cats with celebrity names plaque

segments.voice: Hurricane Irma in 2017 tested the colony's protection protocols. All 60 cats were sheltered inside the main house with staff during the Category 4 storm. Every cat survived and the museum reopened within a week. The hurricane preparedness plan is required by USDA exhibitor regulations.
segments.visual: hemingway house hurricane irma cat protection

segments.voice: Genetic testing for polydactyly became commercially available through Wisdom Panel and Basepaws in 2019. The Pd gene mutation can now be identified before kittens develop visible extra toes. This enables breeding programs to predict trait inheritance with mathematical accuracy.
segments.visual: cat dna testing genetic results polydactyly identification

segments.voice: Polydactyl kittens require slightly modified neonatal care. The extra toes can develop minor circulation issues during the first week if compressed or improperly positioned. Experienced breeders monitor toe development closely during the critical period after birth to ensure healthy formation.
segments.visual: newborn polydactyl kitten care toe development monitoring

segments.voice: Some polydactyl cats develop functional thumbs that enable manipulation behaviors normal cats cannot perform. Documented cases include cats opening doors, manipulating latches, and grasping objects between digits. This dexterity provided real advantages on sailing ships requiring rope and rigging manipulation.
segments.visual: polydactyl cat using thumb to open door manipulate

segments.voice: The mutation is not species-specific to domestic cats. Polydactyly occurs in dogs, mice, humans, and other mammals through related but distinct genetic mechanisms. Human polydactyly affects approximately 1 in 500 births and has been documented since ancient times in medical literature.
segments.visual: polydactyly across species mammalian genetics comparison

segments.voice: Veterinary care for polydactyl cats includes specialized nail trimming. The extra digits often have nails that grow at different rates and angles than standard toes. Without regular maintenance, these nails can curl into the paw pad and require veterinary intervention to prevent infection.
segments.visual: veterinarian trimming polydactyl cat nails careful technique

segments.voice: The Hemingway colony reproduces under controlled circumstances to maintain genetic diversity while preserving the polydactyl trait. Breeding decisions involve coordination with veterinary geneticists to prevent inbreeding while ensuring trait continuation through the colony's future generations.
segments.visual: hemingway colony breeding management genetic diversity

segments.voice: Cat shows in some countries still allow polydactyl cats to compete. The PolyTraks division within The International Cat Association recognizes polydactyl Maine Coons specifically. Other registries like the World Cat Federation maintain stricter standard-toe requirements, varying by international jurisdiction and association politics.
segments.visual: cat show polydactyl competition international cat association

segments.voice: Famous individual polydactyl cats throughout history include Mark Twain's cat Bambino, Sailor Boy of New England, and the original Snow White at Key West. These cats often gained celebrity status due to their unusual appearance and the cultural associations with luck and uniqueness.
segments.visual: famous polydactyl cats throughout history portrait collection

segments.voice: The Hemingway Home Museum welcomes approximately 270,000 visitors annually. The colony tour represents the primary attraction, with cats freely roaming the property and grounds. The cats receive specialized veterinary care, nutritional management, and protected environments that few wild animal populations enjoy globally.
segments.visual: tourists visiting hemingway house with polydactyl cats

segments.voice: Most owners never realize their unusual-looking cat with extra toes may be carrying a genetic legacy traceable to medieval Welsh cats and sailing ship voyages across centuries. The Pd gene survives because humans valued and preserved these cats, making the trait one of the few mutations actively selected for by human cultural preference.
segments.visual: polydactyl cat heritage genetic legacy reveal

segments.voice: Tomorrow's video covers calico and tortoiseshell genetics, including why these cats are almost exclusively female and the rare male cases caused by chromosomal abnormalities. Subscribe to understand the X chromosome inactivation creating these distinctive coat patterns.
segments.visual: calico tortoiseshell cat coat pattern xx chromosome
